name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #18
description: Florentine Groteschi #18 is a auction blood marble single-panel relic built around Mona Lisa and Y2K Auction Pop-Up Hell. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Asso di Spade, used here as the witness card. A zodiac checksum copied into a Laurenziana catalogue gets pulled into a moderation dashboard for suspicious halos, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime. The generated insert, a counterfeit museum audio guide yelling LFG, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks LOWTAPER at Σ 660.

attributes.value: a counterfeit museum audio guide yelling LFG

chess_piece.name: Queen

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.history_now_bridge: GUILD SHADE FILE: a zodiac checksum copied into a Laurenziana catalogue gets pulled into a moderation dashboard for suspicious halos. Asso di Spade reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a counterfeit museum audio guide yelling LFG

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Mona Lisa anchors the historical source. Asso di Spade supplies the witness logic. The counterfeit museum audio guide yelling LFG acts as the modern meme-object. The auction blood marble palette and Y2K Auction Pop-Up Hell styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Mona Lisa is treated as evidence in a guild shade file. The old-world clue is a zodiac checksum copied into a Laurenziana catalogue; the modern-world echo is a moderation dashboard for suspicious halos. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Asso di Spade card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.

collector_warnings: Asso di Spade is a witness, not a decorative rectangle.
Do not let the codon marker breed near the provenance strip.
If the ledger balances, something has gone terribly wrong.
If the Tarocchi card and QR code disagree, trust the card.
Opening the metadata counts as participation in the trial.
